assumptions: The developer does not have a Buildkite account or organization.
No source-code repository, Buildkite agent, pipeline definition, test suite, or paid plan is assumed.

developer_goal: Create a free personal Buildkite account, run the vendor-provided Starter pipeline, and inspect its completed annotation.

first_success.outcome: Create a personal Buildkite account and organization, select the Buildkite Examples Starter pipeline, run its first build on the default hosted environment, wait for completion, and inspect the annotation.
first_success.observable_signal: The Starter build completes and its Annotations tab displays the content of the example repository's .buildkite/annotation.md file.

route_selection.surface: Getting started with Pipelines, stage 1: Buildkite Starter example
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Getting started with Pipelines, stage 1: Buildkite Starter example
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Create your own pipeline
candidates.reason: This is stage 2 of the same tutorial and requires the developer's own repository, task, pipeline.yml, source-control connection, and new build.

candidates.name: Add a Test Engine suite
candidates.reason: This is stage 3 after an operating pipeline and adds framework-specific collector and API-token setup.

candidates.name: Self-hosted agent onboarding
candidates.reason: The personal signup path creates hosted queues and agents, so the Starter example can run before self-hosted installation.

required_path.action: Complete Buildkite account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Buildkite account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Buildkite candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: The Starter build completes and its Annotations tab displays the content of the example repository's .buildkite/annotation.md file.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'identity', 'requirement': 'A GitHub identity that can authorize Buildkite, or an email address, full name, and password for email signup.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}
{'order': 2, 'type': 'access', 'requirement': 'Browser and network access to Buildkite, and email inbox access when choosing email signup.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}

{'order': 3, 'type': 'permission', 'requirement': 'For GitHub signup, permission to authorize Buildkite and, if prompted, install the Buildkite GitHub App for all or selected repositories.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}

external_gates.description: Signup branches between GitHub authorization and email/password plus email verification.
GitHub signup requires authorizing Buildkite to access the account.
Installing the GitHub App in an organization can require repository-owner or administrator permission.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Create a new pipeline from one of the Buildkite-provided examples, to learn the New Pipeline page and see how a build runs.

excluded.item: Creating .buildkite/pipeline.yml in the developer's own repository and committing it.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Connecting a private GitLab, Bitbucket, or other remote repository and configuring webhooks or SSH keys.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Creating the developer's own pipeline and manually starting My first build.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

excluded.item: Creating a Test Engine suite, configuring a framework collector and API token, and automating test results.
excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

sources.title: Getting started with Pipelines
evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

sources.title: Defining your pipeline steps
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for Defining your pipeline steps

uncertainties.question: Does a GitHub organization require administrator approval for the Buildkite App installation?
uncertainties.impact: This can add another actor and wait before New Pipeline.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The guide documents the Install Buildkite prompt and repository-scope choice but cannot know each GitHub organization's App policy.

uncertainties.question: What exact validation, password, and verification-error states occur during current email signup?
uncertainties.impact: Those screens can add retries or prevent account activation.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The official guide lists the fields and verification link but does not enumerate error and recovery states.

uncertainties.question: How long does the hosted Starter build take or queue?
uncertainties.impact: The first-success time includes an unbounded execution wait.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: No duration or service-level claim is provided for this example.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Buildkite show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
